mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged increase in CPI inflation above 2.5% signals central bank inaction on price pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns during monetary tightening regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average indicates expanding manufacturing capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) below 100k indicates weakening labor demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS declines below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A positive divergence between GDP growth (GDPC1) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals favorable macroeconomic conditions for equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-CPIAUCSL divergence fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in 50%+ of cases, or their relationship reverses direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month low signals supply-side distress, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during crisis regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines below its 12-month low.
